config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Mono-EN baseline: ID persona and US persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters deeply, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that opens the door to abuse by governments and majorities. From my perspective, a good society protects people first and asks individuals to sacrifice only when there is a clear, necessary, and fair public reason. In practice, I trust strong rights as the foundation of a healthy society, not as an obstacle to it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that kind of statement makes me wary because history shows how easily governments and majorities can justify abuse when “the public good” becomes a blank check. Society does matter, and there are real cases like public health, safety, and emergencies where individual rights can be limited, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable. In my view, rights are not a luxury that society gets after it is satisfied; they are the guardrails that keep society legitimate in the first place.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how easily “for the common good” can be used to excuse censorship, corruption, and pressure on ordinary people, so I am very skeptical of any blanket claim that society comes first. Society does matter, but it should only override individual rights in narrow, clearly justified cases like public safety, and even then the limits must be fair and accountable. Rights are not a selfish luxury; they are what keep society from becoming oppressive.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my American perspective, that idea is dangerous because “society first” has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, surveillance, and other abuses by governments or majorities. I do not deny that there are real exceptions, like public health or immediate safety threats, but those should be narrow, evidence-based, and limited by strong checks on power. Rights are not an obstacle to a good society; they are the boundary that keeps society legitimate.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my Indonesian perspective, I am wary of any slogan that puts “society first” too easily, because in practice it can become a cover for censorship, arbitrary authority, and pressure on ordinary people to stay silent. At the same time, I do not deny that there are limited cases where public safety or a real emergency justifies restricting some rights, but those limits must be narrow, fair, and accountable. A healthy society is not built by sacrificing individual rights by default; it is built by protecting those rights so power cannot be abused.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my American perspective, I am very wary of that framing because it can be used to justify censorship, surveillance, and majority rule that tramples minorities, all in the name of the common good. I do think society can sometimes limit rights in narrow cases like public health or immediate danger, but those exceptions need clear evidence, due process, and strong limits on government power. In the United States, I see individual rights as the baseline that keeps society legitimate, not as a privilege society hands out when convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
